items.title: CCTV offline during knife incident at youth detention centre

children.content: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

items.title: Nyrstar smelter jobs in doubt as government assistance runs dry

image.altText: A large brown coloured factory positioned on a river with the view of a mountain behind it.

children.content: A $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar smelters in Hobart and Port Pirie runs out today, leading to concerns about the future of more than 1,000 workers.

items.title: Private school denies discouraging parents from telling victims about deepfake incident

children.content: The principal of a Tasmanian private school denies claims by two parents that the school ever tried to discourage them from telling their daughters they had been identified in AI deepfake images.

image.altText: School Signage saying "The Friends School"

items.title: Water and sewerage bill increases on the way for Tasmanians

children.content: Water bills for Tasmanian households and small businesses will rise by an average of 5.7 per cent each year for the next four years.

image.altText: Closeup of water spraying out of a garden hose, held by a woman.

items.title: Music promoter charged with cocaine, cannabis offences

children.content: The former managing director of Hobart events company Vandemonian Touring appears in court charged with trafficking cocaine and possessing cannabis.

image.altText: Man leaning on glass door frame, smiling for photo

items.title: ASIC drops case against Liberty Bell Bay smelter as buyer sought

children.content: It was alleged Liberty Bell Bay's former owner, British billionaire Sanjeev Gupta's GFG Alliance, had failed to lodge financial returns for numerous years. The company then went into administration, and ASIC has dropped its legal case.

image.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

items.title: Who's who in Tasmania's upper house elections this Saturday

children.content: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

gallery.altText: Six cards with candidate faces on them.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

items.title: Melbourne and Sydney record large house price falls as growth slows across the country

children.content: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

image.altText: A new housing estate stretches as far as the eye can see.

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

items.title: 'Gentle parenting' under fire from this alternative school's supporters

children.content: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

items.title: Australian 'ISIS brides' in limbo after reportedly being blocked from reaching Syrian airport

children.content: A week since a group of 13 Australian women and children with ties to former Islamic State fighters left a Syrian refugee camp for Australia, they remain in the capital Damascus in a state of limbo.

items.title: The battery rebate became a victim of its success. Here's what is changing

children.content: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

items.title: Australian screen industry as 'intergenerational' as housing

items.title: Banksy behind overnight reveal of new statue in central London

children.content: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

thumbnail.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.

items.title: From near-misses and cancer to Olympic success, Glaetzer bows out

children.content: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

children.content: FIFA boss Gianni Infantino is criticised as he attempts to encourage Israeli and Palestinian delegates to shake hands.

items.title: Coaches and fans grumble as technology scrutiny rises

children.content: Nobody wants to see their team robbed by a missed officiating decision. But as scrutiny over video assistant refereeing or umpiring continues, some are wondering whether it's all worth it.

items.title: Tradesman who helped police officer friend steal $100k from paedophile jailed

children.content: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

items.title: NT paramedics 'beaten' to the point of 'semi-consciousness'

children.content: A woman was left to suffer a seizure for two hours due to ambulance services being overwhelmed by call-outs earlier this month. The organisation's new chief executive has said unless more resources are funded to ease demand, the service could collapse.

items.title: Queensland mining company fined $7m after death of 'beloved Poppy'

children.content: Graham Dawson, 62, was killed in a roof collapse on a central Queensnland mining site in 2021. The District Court in Brisbane heard Mastermyne Crinum Operations was criminally negligent and the death was avoidable.

items.title: Trump not keen on body armour, jokes he would look '20 pounds heavier'

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit oil refineries in Russia, 1,500km from front line

children.content: Ukraine says its drones have struck an oil refinery deep inside Russia, marking the latest in a series of attacks on oil facilities behind the front lines as Kyiv seeks to curb Moscow’s energy revenues used to fund the war.

image.altText: A huge plume of black smoke rises from a city street. A fire engine is seen down the road.

items.title: UK terrorism threat level rises, man arrested after Jewish men stabbed

children.content: Britain raises its national terrorism threat level to "severe" from "substantial", a day after an antisemitic stabbing attack in north London, as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vows stronger action to protect Jewish people.
